text.tspan: Most LLM calls share a HUGE unchanging prefix (system prompt, docs, tools, examples).
Without caching: that prefix is RE-PROCESSED on every single call. Expensive and slow.
With caching: the provider stores the KV cache for the prefix and reuses it.
Call 1: [10k system + tools] + 'hello' -> 10,003 input tokens billed
Call 2: [10k system + tools] + 'goodbye' -> 10,003 input tokens billed
...100 calls = 1M input tokens billed
Call 1: WRITE 10k cache (1.25x cost) + 'hello' -> 12,503 tokens billed
Call 2: READ 10k cache (0.1x cost) + 'goodbye' -> 1,003 tokens billed
...100 calls = 12,500 + 99x 1,003 = ~111k input tokens billed
~90% cost reduction. >2x latency reduction.
Cache valid for 5 min (Anthropic) or longer.
Anthropic (Aug 2024): explicit cache_control breakpoints.
Gemini (Feb 2024): context caching API; longer TTL.
OpenAI (Oct 2024): automatic for >1024 tokens prefix.
All providers: cache key = prefix HASH. Any change invalidates.
- Coding agents (huge unchanging codebase context)
- Customer support (unchanging KB + tools)
- RAG systems (cache the system prompt + tools)
- Long documents (CAG: cache the whole doc instead of RAG)
- Few-shot heavy prompts (cache the examples)
